User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the UniEats system, including clients (students, faculty, administrative staff) and internal staff (cashier, kitchen, administration). This entity supports different roles to manage access and functionality.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

institutionalEmail.description: The institutional email of the user, used for login and identification.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system, determining their permissions and access. Examples include 'student', 'professor', 'admin-client', 'cashier', 'kitchen', 'admin-staff'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines a classification for menu items, helping to organize the menu for customers and staff (e.g., 'Main Courses', 'Drinks', 'Desserts').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a specific food or drink item available for purchase in the cafeteria menu, including its details and categorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item (e.g., 'Chicken Sandwich', 'Coffee').

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, including ingredients or preparation.

price.description: The current selling price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: A URL to an image of the menu item for display in the frontend.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this menu item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N MenuItem)

isAvailable.description: A boolean indicating whether the item is currently available for ordering.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the menu item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the menu item's details were updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's placed order, containing general information about the order, its status, and pickup details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

pickupTime.description: The scheduled date and time for the customer to pick up the order.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'preparing', 'ready for pickup', 'picked up', 'cancelled').

notes.description: Any general notes or special instructions for the entire order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the order's status or details were updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific item within a customer's order, detailing the quantity and price at the time of purchase, including any item-specific notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem that was ordered. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of this menu item included in the order.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the menu item at the time the order was placed.

notes.description: Any specific customization notes for this particular item (e.g., 'no onions', 'extra cheese').

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Tracks the stock levels of a specific item or ingredient in the cafeteria's inventory, enabling inventory control and low-stock alerts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem this inventory item is associated with. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:1 InventoryItem - simplified for direct stock tracking of a menu product).

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the inventory item in stock.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum stock quantity at which an alert should be triggered.

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the stock is measured (e.g., 'grams', 'liters', 'units', 'pieces').

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the inventory item's stock or details were last updated.

InventoryTransaction.title: InventoryTransaction
InventoryTransaction.description: Records every movement (e.g., incoming, outgoing, adjustment) of an inventory item, providing an audit trail for stock changes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryTransaction entity.

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem affected by this transaction. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N InventoryTransaction)

transactionType.description: The type of inventory transaction (e.g., 'RECEIPT' for incoming stock, 'ISSUE' for outgoing stock, 'ADJUSTMENT' for corrections, 'SPOILAGE').

quantity.description: The quantity of the inventory item involved in the transaction. Positive for additions, negative for removals.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

userId.description: Reference to the User (typically staff) who performed or recorded this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N InventoryTransaction)

notes.description: Any additional notes or reasons for the inventory transaction.

RatingAndComment.title: RatingAndComment
RatingAndComment.description: Allows customers to submit ratings and comments for menu items or their overall cafeteria experience, contributing to feedback and recommendation systems.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RatingAndComment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the rating/comment. (Relationship: User 1:N RatingAndComment)

menuItemId.description: Optional reference to the MenuItem that was rated or commented on. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N RatingAndComment)

orderId.description: Optional reference to a specific Order associated with the feedback. This allows rating the overall order experience. (Relationship: Order 1:N RatingAndComment)

rating.description: A numerical rating, typically on a scale (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The textual feedback or comment provided by the user.

commentDate.description: The date and time when the rating and comment were submitted.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Users can typically read and update their own profiles. Admin-staff can manage all user profiles. Includes the user's assigned role. This collection is for profile data, with separate collections for explicit role checks for permissions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to grant and verify 'admin-staff' privileges. The existence of a document for a userId in this collection indicates the user is an administrator with full staff permissions. Used for efficient 'existence' checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID) who has the 'admin-staff' role.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to grant and verify 'cashier' privileges. The existence of a document for a userId in this collection indicates the user is a cashier. Used for efficient 'existence' checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID) who has the 'cashier' role.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to grant and verify 'kitchen' privileges. The existence of a document for a userId in this collection indicates the user works in the kitchen. Used for efficient 'existence' checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID) who has the 'kitchen' role.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to grant and verify 'admin-client' privileges for users who are clients. The existence of a document for a userId in this collection indicates the user has specific elevated client permissions. Used for efficient 'existence' checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID) who has the 'admin-client' role.

definition.description: Defines menu item categories. These documents are publicly readable by all users, allowing for dynamic menu browsing. Writes (create, update, delete) are restricted to 'admin-staff' users.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu category.

definition.description: Contains details for all menu items. These documents are publicly readable by all users. Writes (create, update, delete) are restricted to 'admin-staff' users. Includes `categoryId` as a reference.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Stores all customer orders. Clients can create and read their own orders by filtering on `userId`. Staff (cashier, kitchen) and admin-staff can read all orders and update specific fields like `status`. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Represents individual items within a specific order. Access is tied to the parent order's permissions. Includes denormalized 'userId' (owner of the parent order) for authorization independence without requiring a parent `get()` call.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Tracks stock levels for menu-related ingredients or products. This collection is only accessible (read/write) by 'kitchen' and 'admin-staff' roles, ensuring strict internal control.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inventory item.

definition.description: Records all inventory movements (e.g., receipts, issues, adjustments) for a specific inventory item. These transactions are read-only after creation and accessible only by 'kitchen' and 'admin-staff' roles. Includes `userId` for audit purposes.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent inventory item.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the inventory transaction.

definition.description: Stores customer ratings and comments. All ratings are publicly readable. Clients can create, update, and delete only their own ratings. 'Admin-staff' can read and delete any rating. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: ratingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the rating and comment.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the provided CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. It leverages structural segregation and denormalization for authorization independence, enabling simple and robust security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure ensures Authorization Independence by denormalizing crucial authorization context directly into the documents where it's needed. For example, the `userId` field is explicitly included in `Order`, `OrderItem`, and `RatingAndComment` documents. This allows security rules to check ownership (e.g., `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) without requiring expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents or separate user profiles. This strategy is vital for maintaining atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:** To support secure `list` operations (QAPs) and handle different security postures, the design employs Structural Segregation. Rather than nesting all data under a user's private path, which would prevent staff from listing all orders or administrators from listing all categories, we utilize top-level collections for entities that require broader visibility while still enforcing strict access controls:

*   **Public/Global Data:** Collections like `/categories` and `/menuItems` are top-level and publicly readable, enabling any client to `list` them. Writes are restricted to specific staff roles.
*   **User-Owned Data with Staff Management:** The `/orders` and `/ratings` collections are top-level. This allows staff roles (Cashier, Kitchen, Admin-Staff) to efficiently `list` and manage all orders or reviews, while clients can only `list` or interact with their own documents by querying against the denormalized `userId` field (`where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`).
*   **Staff-Only Data:** Collections like `/inventoryItems` and their subcollections are strictly accessible only by authorized staff roles, ensuring data privacy and integrity.
*   **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** For staff and privileged client roles (`admin-staff`, `cashier`, `kitchen`, `admin-client`), dedicated top-level collections like `/roles_adminStaff/{userId}` are used. The *existence* of a document in these collections grants the respective role. This 'Existence over Content' approach completely eliminates `get()` calls for role checks in security rules, making them highly efficient and easy to reason about (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_adminStaff/$(request.auth.uid))`).

**Clarity of Intent and Predictability:** The use of descriptive collection names and parameters (e.g., `users/{userId}`, `orders/{orderId}`, `menuItems/{menuItemId}`) makes the purpose and ownership of data immediately clear. Consistent field names like `userId` for ownership are standardized across denormalized fields. The system clearly separates responsibilities, making security rules straightforward to write and audit, thereby minimizing complexity and enhancing debuggability.
